name: browser-testing-with-devtools
tagline: Browser-based testing and debugging via Chrome DevTools MCP, with explicit rules treating all page content as untrusted.
verdict: Strong, well-considered security posture — explicit prompt-injection, credential-isolation, and profile-isolation guardrails — plus clear triggers and worked examples. Minor gaps: an unpinned '@latest' MCP package, no fallback if the MCP server itself is unavailable, a long single-file SKILL.md with inline bulk, and no version/changelog signal.

highlights.summary: Well-known official package but installed unpinned at @latest.
Requirement is stated but no step tells the agent what to do if the MCP server is missing or unreachable, unlike the thorough app-level failure diagnosis elsewhere.
A full worked test-plan template and several catalog-like sections (console patterns, a11y checklist) sit inline in a 318-line SKILL.md instead of an on-demand reference file.
